title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Justin Slaten struggled in the seventh, giving up four runs and four hits in Red Sox's 8-6 loss to Twins.
Payton Tolle exited after six innings with nine strikeouts, but manager Chad Tracy opted for the bullpen.
The Red Sox offense scored six runs, but their efforts were overshadowed by the Twins' stronger offensive showing.

title: Roger Clemens throws out first pitch at Fenway Park to son, Twins player Kody Clemens
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roger Clemens threw the first pitch at Fenway Park to his son, Kody Clemens, before a Red Sox-Twins game.
Roger Clemens wore a custom Red Sox jersey and participated in a bobblehead giveaway celebrating his 20-strikeout games.
Kody Clemens, playing for the Twins, hit a home run in his first game at Fenway last year with his father present.

title: Celtics’ Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Derrick White was named to the 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team, his first career nod.
White recorded career highs with 98 blocks and 88 steals, leading all guards in defensive impact.
The Celtics finished second in the Eastern Conference with a 56-26 record despite an early playoff exit.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Glory,' an Oscar-winning Civil War drama, is available on Netflix until June 1.
'Arco,' a French animated film featuring an all-star English dub cast, is streaming on Hulu.
Tatiana Maslany stars in 'Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed,' a crime drama available on Apple TV.

title: Chad Tracy provides injury updates for Garrett Crochet, Roman Anthony
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox manager Chad Tracy updates on Garrett Crochet and Roman Anthony's injuries; no return timeline set.
Garrett Crochet to have a throwing session and live batting practice next week, progressing towards return.
Roman Anthony struggles with pain tolerance post-swing, no new injuries but return delayed beyond initial expectation.

title: Sam Kennedy says Red Sox looking to add bat ‘earlier than ever’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox President Sam Kennedy says team is discussing trading for a hitter earlier than usual this season.
Boston struggles offensively with 181 runs, second-least in MLB, and only 36 home runs, second-lowest in league.
Team aims to improve lineup amid injuries, including Trevor Story's hernia surgery and struggles in scoring runs.

title: Mock draft predicts Red Sox will select Bishop Feehan star in first round
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Mock draft by MLB.com's Jonathan Mayo predicts Red Sox will select Bishop Feehan's Brody Bumila at No. 20.
Brody Bumila, a 6-foot-9 lefty, is receiving attention for his rapid fastball and potential as a starter.
Scouts showed significant interest in Bumila, but concerns include his limited offspeed pitches and past UCL injury.

title: Red Sox’ Trevor Story undergoes sports hernia surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox shortstop Trevor Story underwent successful sports hernia surgery and is expected to miss 6-10 weeks.
Story has struggled with injuries since joining the Red Sox, having undergone three surgeries including this hernia operation.
In Story's absence, Isaiah Kiner-Falefa and Andruw Monasterio will fill in, with Marcelo Mayer expected to start at shortstop.

title: Peter Wolf left one Boston art school to form the J. Geils Band. Six decades later, he earned an honorary doctorate from another.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Peter Wolf, former frontman of the J. Geils Band, received an honorary doctorate from Massachusetts College of Art and Design.
Wolf played key roles in the art and music scene, sharing a Boston apartment with filmmaker David Lynch in the 1960s.
Wolf's memoir, 'Waiting on the Moon,' includes stories of encounters with Bob Dylan, Marilyn Monroe, and Bruce Springsteen's manager.

title: Boston 25 hires Lauren Walsh for newly-created role
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Lauren Walsh joins Boston 25 as a sports media journalist, previously worked at WSMV in Nashville.
Walsh is a Medford native and Syracuse graduate, with past roles in Winston-Salem, Burlington, and Syracuse.
Boston 25 has created a new position for Walsh, enhancing their sports coverage with her storytelling skills.
